criteria.good: Every P0 evidence feature appears as a named part_tree child or a geometry_ops target.
criteria.bad: Subsystem claims features in prose but leaves no geometry target or child node.

criteria.good: Coordinates span stations, ports, bearing seats, service faces, or datum pads.
criteria.bad: All geometry is stacked at the origin or represented as one box/cylinder.

criteria.good: Uses pockets, channels, bosses, bolt patterns, ribs, seals, and inspection covers.
criteria.bad: Only primitive body solids with no interface or service evidence.

criteria.good: Each adjacent interface names a joint family, DOF, clearance, datum, and mating feature.
criteria.bad: Interfaces are vague mounts with no reciprocal feature or clearance.

criteria.good: Wear parts, covers, plugs, connectors, seals, or cartridges have visible removal paths.
criteria.bad: Maintenance-critical items are trapped behind sealed geometry.

criteria.good: The main load, torque, heat, fluid, or current path is traceable through named features.
criteria.bad: Functional flow/load claims stop at a shell without interfaces.

criteria.good: Uses subsystem-specific evidence features: turbine_shell, compressor_shell, oil_feed_boss, charge_pipe_flange, heat_shield.
criteria.bad: Omits the visible grammar for turbo_air_exhaust_study and substitutes generic blocks.

criteria.good: Includes service, inspection, sealing, datum, or safety-boundary evidence where relevant.
criteria.bad: Claims a functional subsystem without access, datums, labels, or boundary constraints.

operability_profile.operating_principle: Diesel engine architecture study with reinforced block/head, heavy cranktrain, common-rail topology, turbo air path, cooling/lubrication, and service datums.
